population_setting: adults with OSA from SHHS without pre-existing atrial fibrillation
osa_definition: OSA participants from SHHS without baseline atrial fibrillation
exposure_definition: hypoxic burden quartiles
outcome_definition: first incident atrial fibrillation during follow-up
adjustment_summary: multivariable model adjusting for major demographic and cardiovascular covariates
sleep_study_type: home polysomnography in the Sleep Heart Health Study
source_location: Results in 1-s2.0-S1389945725001339-main.pdf; HB Q4 vs Q1 incident AF
notes: Overlap-prone SHHS reuse with OR-based AF modeling. Retain as sensitivity/comparator evidence rather than as a primary AF anchor.

population_setting: adults with OSA undergoing major noncardiothoracic surgery
osa_definition: adults with diagnosed OSA undergoing major noncardiothoracic surgery
exposure_definition: sleep apnea-specific hypoxic burden tertiles
outcome_definition: composite of stroke, atrial fibrillation, heart failure, myocardial infarction, venous thromboembolism, and all-cause mortality within 30 days of surgery
adjustment_summary: multivariable perioperative model including age, emergency admission, and surgical-risk covariates
sleep_study_type: preoperative diagnostic sleep study linked to administrative surgical outcomes
notes: Specialized perioperative translational cohort that thickens the SASHB hard-outcome layer, but the 30-day postoperative composite is not directly comparable with chronic OSA prognostic cells.

population_setting: adults hospitalized with recent myocardial infarction and post-event PSG
osa_definition: post-MI adults without prior sleep-apnea diagnosis; central apnea and CPAP users excluded
exposure_definition: nocturnal minimum oxygen saturation <=85%
outcome_definition: major adverse cardiac events after myocardial infarction
model_used: multivariable Cox model with landmark analysis >=15 months
adjustment_summary: age, left ventricular ejection fraction, diabetes mellitus, oxygen desaturation index, and arousal index
sleep_study_type: full-night polysomnography after recent myocardial infarction
notes: Specialized recent-post-MI cohort with a strong nadir oxygen saturation signal, but event-window landmarking and cardiac-population framing keep it in the sensitivity/comparator layer.

population_setting: adults hospitalized with recent myocardial infarction and post-event PSG

outcome_definition: major adverse cardiac events after myocardial infarction

population_setting: community-based adults with complete baseline and follow-up data
